name: Hash Functions
creator: NSA (SHA), NIST, Guido Bertoni et al. (SHA-3/Keccak)
breakability: MD5 and SHA-1 are broken (collision attacks). SHA-256+ remain secure.
modern_use: Password storage (with salt), file integrity, digital signatures, blockchain, deduplication
description: Cryptographic hash functions transform arbitrary-length input into a fixed-size digest. Properties: pre-image resistance (cannot reverse), second pre-image resistance (cannot find same hash for different input), collision resistance (cannot find two inputs with same hash). SHA-3 uses Keccak sponge construction, different from SHA-2.
key_sizes: No keys - hash functions are unkeyed
notes: Never use MD5 or SHA-1 for security purposes. For password hashing, use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2 (not raw SHA).
did_you_know: The SHA-1 algorithm was officially broken in 2017 with the SHAttered attack, which found the first known SHA-1 collision using 9.2 quintillion SHA-1 computations.
